cards.q: What problem does sparse attention solve?
cards.a: Standard attention scales quadratically with sequence length, making it too slow and memory-intensive for long documents. Sparse attention reduces this to near-linear complexity.

cards.q: What is the complexity of window-based attention?
cards.a: O of n times w, where n is sequence length and w is the window size. This compares to O of n squared for standard attention.

cards.q: Why does sparse attention lose so little quality despite computing fewer interactions?
cards.a: Because most attention weights are near zero in practice. Sparse attention simply skips the interactions that would have had negligible weight anyway.
